message:
chore(beads): add beads for manifest SHA256 drift and ARM64 meta_skill

bd-38l7j: Regenerate manifest_index.sh (issue #116)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-33vh.8","title":"End-to-end test: Fresh install + doctor confirms DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings","description":"## Task

Run a complete end-to-end test to verify complete git_safety_guard removal with DETAILED LOGGING.

## Test Script Location

Create: tests/e2e/test_git_safety_guard_removal.sh

## Logging Requirements

ALL tests MUST:
1. Log to timestamped file: /tmp/git_safety_guard_removal_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log
2. Structured format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [TEST_NAME] Message
3. JSON results file

## Test Environment

Use Docker-based test harness: ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh

## Test Cases

### Test Case 1: Fresh Install Verification
```bash
# After fresh install in Docker
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/              # Should only show settings.json
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ 2>&1   # Should fail (directory doesn't exist)
ls -la ~/.claude/hooks/ 2>&1        # Should fail or be empty
```

### Test Case 2: Doctor Output Verification
```bash
acfs doctor 2>&1 | tee doctor_output.txt

# Should NOT contain
! grep -i 'git.safety.guard' doctor_output.txt

# Should contain DCG check
grep -i 'DCG' doctor_output.txt
```

### Test Case 3: Install Log Verification
- Should see: 'Installing DCG' or similar
- Should NOT see: 'Git Safety Guard'

### Test Case 4: Settings.json Verification
```bash
cat ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null
# Should NOT contain 'git_safety_guard'
```

### Test Case 5: Codebase Audit
```bash
# No git_safety_guard in codebase
! grep -ri 'git_safety_guard' --include='*.sh' --include='*.ts' --include='*.md' .
```

## Expected Results Table

| Check | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ exists | NO |
| ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py exists | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'Git safety guard' | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'DCG' | YES |
| install.sh log mentions 'Git Safety Guard' | NO |
| install.sh log mentions 'DCG' | YES |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Docker-based fresh install completes successfully
2. No git_safety_guard warnings in doctor output
3. DCG check present and passes
4. Install logs use 'DCG' terminology
5. No stale artifacts created
6. Detailed timestamped log file generated
7. JSON results file with pass/fail summary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:16.986042720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T00:53:52.224631333Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T00:53:52.224494725Z","close_reason":"Created E2E test for git_safety_guard removal with Docker integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","qa","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:16.986042720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:06.307562642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:09.087861241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:11.715678744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.9","title":"Document migration path for existing users in CHANGELOG or release notes","description":"## Task Overview

Document the git_safety_guard → DCG migration for users who may be upgrading from older ACFS installations.

## Target Audience

Users who installed ACFS before January 11, 2026 (commit f1fd501) and may:
1. See 'Git safety guard' warnings in acfs doctor
2. Have stale git_safety_guard.py files
3. Be confused about the difference between the old and new systems

## Documentation Locations

### Option 1: Add to CHANGELOG.md (if exists)
```markdown
## [0.x.x] - 2026-01-xx

### Changed
- **BREAKING**: git_safety_guard replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard)

### Migration
If you see 'Git safety guard' warnings after updating:
1. Run `acfs update` to get the latest scripts
2. Remove legacy files: `rm -f ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py`
3. Install DCG: `dcg install`
4. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show DCG check, not git_safety_guard
```

### Option 2: Add migration section to README.md
```markdown
## Upgrading from Pre-January 2026 Installations

If you installed ACFS before January 11, 2026, you may have the old git_safety_guard system.
The replacement is DCG (Destructive Command Guard).

### Symptoms of Old Installation
- `acfs doctor` shows 'Git safety guard' warning
- File exists: ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py

### Migration Steps
1. Update ACFS: `acfs update --force`
2. Install DCG: `dcg install`
3. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show only DCG

The update process automatically cleans up legacy git_safety_guard files.
```

### Option 3: In-tool messaging (services-setup.sh)
When user runs `acfs services-setup`, if legacy files detected:
```
⚠️  Legacy git_safety_guard detected
   This has been replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard).
   
   Cleaning up old files...
   ✓ Removed ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py
   
   To configure DCG, select 'DCG' from the menu or run: dcg install
```

## Recommended Approach

Combine Options 1 and 3:
1. Document in CHANGELOG for release notes
2. Add proactive cleanup messaging in services-setup.sh

## Content Requirements

Documentation should explain:
1. **What changed**: git_safety_guard.py removed, DCG introduced
2. **Why**: Better performance (Rust vs Python), modular packs, dedicated maintenance
3. **How to migrate**: Run acfs update, install dcg
4. **How to verify**: acfs doctor shows DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings
5. **Timeline**: When this changed (January 11, 2026)

## Notes

- Keep migration docs concise - most users won't need them
- Focus on actionable steps, not history
- Link to DCG section in README for full documentation","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T00:33:17.644632289Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T00:33:17.643021004Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["documentation","migration","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:03.529936278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-38l7j","title":"Regenerate manifest_index.sh to fix SHA256 drift","description":"scripts/generated/manifest_index.sh is out of sync with acfs.manifest.yaml. The manifest was modified in commits 44852195 (tru→toon) and 0a64aa63 (revert toon→tru) but manifest_index.sh was never regenerated.

Current state:
- manifest_index.sh line 1 has stale hash d7db51f0...
- manifest_index.sh has a DUPLICATE ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256 line with hash 3f916ff3...
- Actual manifest SHA256 at HEAD is 3183c594...
- Bootstrap validation fails: grep ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256 | head -n 1 picks the stale value
- Users pinning ACFS_REF=v0.6.0 get validation failures

Fix:
1. Regenerate manifest_index.sh using the manifest tooling (likely bun run generate or similar)
2. Remove the duplicate ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256 line
3. Verify bootstrap validation works with the regenerated file
4. Test with pinned refs (ACFS_REF=v0.6.0) to ensure no mixed-ref issues

Key files: acfs.manifest.yaml, scripts/generated/manifest_index.sh, scripts/lib/ (generation tooling)

 {"id":"bd-39ye","title":"NO_COLOR environment variable support","description":"## Overview
Respect the NO_COLOR environment variable across all ACFS scripts per https://no-color.org/ standard.

## Current Problem
- Scripts use colors unconditionally
- Users with accessibility needs can't disable colors
- Piped output includes ANSI codes
- Some terminals render colors poorly

## NO_COLOR Standard
- If NO_COLOR env var is set (any value), disable colors
- Also disable for non-TTY output (pipes, redirects)
- Simple, widely adopted convention

## Implementation Details
1. Create color helper functions in logging.sh
2. Check NO_COLOR and TTY status once at startup
3. All color output goes through these helpers

## Color Helper Functions
```bash
# scripts/lib/colors.sh
_init_colors() {
  if [[ -n "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] || [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' BLUE='' RESET=''
  else
    RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' BLUE='\033[0;34m' RESET='\033[0m'
  fi
}

color_print() {
  local color="$1" msg="$2"
  printf '%b%s%b\n' "${!color}" "$msg" "$RESET"
}
```

## Files to Audit
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (main color usage)
- scripts/lib/tui.sh (interactive elements)
- scripts/install.sh (progress output)
- All scripts that use printf with ANSI codes

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR=1 disables all colors
- [ ] Test piped output has no ANSI codes
- [ ] Test colors work normally when NO_COLOR unset
- [ ] Grep for raw ANSI codes to ensure none slip through

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (add color helpers)
- All files using hardcoded ANSI codes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:49.345301724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T04:01:25.739562837Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T04:01:25.739539473Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ye","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:01.194662910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3aa6","title":"Prevent gcloud 'bv' from ever shadowing beads_viewer","description":"Summary
- gcloud SDK installs a `bv` binary in `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin`.
- The SDK’s `path.zsh.inc` is sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`, so PATH can include gcloud’s `bv`.
- User requirement: **gcloud’s `bv` must never, under any circumstances, intercept/be invoked instead of beads_viewer `bv`.**

Impact
- High risk of running the wrong `bv` in interactive shells, non-interactive shells, CI, or cron jobs.
- Mis-executed `bv` can break bead workflows and cause confusion during incident response.

Evidence (current environment)
- `which -a bv` shows multiple `bv` binaries including gcloud’s:
  - `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/go/bin/bv`
- PATH is mutated by gcloud SDK via `path.zsh.inc` (sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`).

Root Cause
- gcloud SDK ships a `bv` command (BigQuery-related) that collides with beads_viewer’s `bv`.
- PATH ordering is not explicitly pinned to prefer user `bv` binaries.

Proposed Remediation (must enforce precedence)
1) Prepend user bins ahead of gcloud in shell init:
   - `~/bin`, `~/.local/bin`, `~/.bun/bin`, `~/go/bin` must come before the SDK.
2) Add an explicit `bv` shim at `~/bin/bv` that delegates to the preferred beads_viewer binary.
3) Add a hard alias in shell init to force `bv` -> `~/.local/bin/bv` (or preferred).
4) Add a health check command (or script) that fails if `command -v bv` resolves to gcloud.

Acceptance Criteria
- `command -v bv` resolves to user beads_viewer (not gcloud) in:
  - interactive shells
  - non-interactive shells (`zsh -lc`, cron)
- `which -a bv` shows gcloud’s `bv` only after user paths.
- Running `bv --version` matches beads_viewer’s version, not gcloud’s.

Notes
- The collision warning appears after `gcloud components update`.
- This is a safety/operations issue, not a GA4 issue.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:42:06.371685930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:22:24.883957326Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:22:24.883935966Z","close_reason":"Implemented bv() protection function in acfs.zshrc that bypasses gcloud bv, added ~/bin to PATH, and enhanced doctor.sh to detect gcloud shadowing. Shellcheck passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3co7k","title":"World-Class UI/UX Polish","description":"# World-Class UI/UX Polish Initiative

## Overview
Elevate the ACFS web application from B+ (78/100) to A+ (95/100) grade UI/UX quality, matching Stripe-level polish and sophistication.

## Background & Context
The web app (apps/web/) is a Next.js 16 application using:
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Framer Motion for animations  
- shadcn/ui component patterns
- OKLCH color space for perceptually uniform colors

**Current State (After Initial Session):**
- ✅ Touch targets meet Apple HIG (44px minimum)
- ✅ Glassmorphism refined with hover states (blur 16px → 20px on hover)
- ✅ Card shadows use layered elevation (Stripe-style)
- ✅ Reduced motion support (useReducedMotion throughout)
- ✅ EmptyState component created with staggered animations
- ✅ 4-column grids on widescreen (xl:grid-cols-4)
- ✅ CodeBlock with line hover highlighting
- ✅ Gradient button variants added
- ✅ Z-index normalized to standard scale

**Remaining Work:**
1. Design System Foundation (typography, animation variants)
2. Core Components (BottomSheet, FormField, Alerts)
3. Page Enhancements (scroll reveals, empty states, swipe)
4. Mobile Experience (bottom sheets, navigation)

## Goals
1. **Desktop Excellence**: Micro-interactions that feel delightful
2. **Mobile Excellence**: Native-feeling gestures and thumb-friendly layouts
3. **Visual Sophistication**: Depth, layering, attention to detail
4. **Performance**: Smooth 60fps animations, no jank
5. **Accessibility**: WCAG AA compliance maintained

## Success Criteria
- Every interaction feels intentional and crafted
- Mobile feels like a native app, not responsive website
- Loading states are elegant, not just functional
- Empty states are delightful, not disappointing

## Key Files
- apps/web/components/ui/ - Core UI components
- apps/web/components/motion/ - Animation system
- apps/web/lib/design-tokens.ts - Design tokens
- apps/web/app/globals.css - Global styles
- apps/web/lib/hooks/useScrollReveal.ts - Scroll animations

## Reference Standards
- Stripe Dashboard, Linear App, Vercel Dashboard
- Apple Human Interface Guidelines","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:53:00.455976257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:55:08.197078236Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:55:08.197057658Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","polish","ui","ux"],"comments":[{"id":53,"issue_id":"bd-3co7k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Strategy Overview

### Unit Testing Approach
- Each new component gets `__tests__/<component>.test.tsx`
- Tests verify: render, props handling, state transitions, ARIA attributes
- Use Jest + React Testing Library
- Mock framer-motion for unit tests when needed

### E2E Testing Approach
- Each feature gets `e2e/<feature>.spec.ts`
- Tests include detailed `console.log('[E2E]...')` logging
- Test reduced motion with `page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: 'reduce' })`
- Test mobile with `page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })`
- Test touch gestures with `page.mouse` swipe simulation

### Test File Locations
```
apps/web/
├── components/
│   ├── ui/__tests__/
│   │   ├── bottom-sheet.test.tsx
│   │   ├── form-field.test.tsx
│   │   └── typography.test.tsx
│   └── motion/__tests__/
│       └── variants.test.ts
├── lib/hooks/__tests__/
│   ├── useScrollReveal.test.ts
│   └── useSwipeScroll.test.ts
└── e2e/
    ├── typography.spec.ts
    ├── animations.spec.ts
    ├── bottom-sheet.spec.ts
    ├── form-field.spec.ts
    ├── alert-card.spec.ts
    ├── button-loading.spec.ts
    ├── scroll-reveal.spec.ts
    ├── empty-states.spec.ts
    ├── swipe-scroll.spec.ts
    ├── jargon-mobile.spec.ts
    └── mobile-navigation.spec.ts
```

### Verification Commands
```bash
# Run unit tests
pnpm --filter @acfs/web test

# Run E2E tests
pnpm --filter @acfs/web e2e

# Run specific E2E test with logging
pnpm --filter @acfs/web e2e -- --grep "BottomSheet"
```

 {"id":"bd-3moq","title":"Subtask: Add remediate() functions to doctor checks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.953602121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:38.331339230Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:38.331179739Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-31ps.6.2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3nbx","title":"acfs doctor --fix: Auto-remediation mode","description":"## Overview
Add `--fix` flag to `acfs doctor` that automatically remediates detected issues instead of just reporting them.

## Current Behavior
- `acfs doctor` runs health checks and reports problems
- User must manually fix each issue based on suggestions
- High friction for beginners who don't know HOW to fix things

## Proposed Behavior
- `acfs doctor --fix` attempts automatic remediation
- Safe fixes only: reinstall tools, fix permissions, regenerate configs
- Never destructive: won't delete user data or change system settings
- Falls back to manual instructions for unsafe operations

## Implementation Details
1. Modify `scripts/lib/doctor.sh` check functions to return remediation commands
2. Add `--fix` flag parsing to `scripts/commands/doctor.sh`
3. Each check needs: detect(), describe(), remediate()
4. Remediation functions wrapped in confirmation prompts
5. Dry-run mode: `--fix --dry-run` shows what would be done

## Safety Constraints
- NEVER delete user files
- NEVER modify ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc without backup
- Always confirm before system-level changes
- Log all remediation actions to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log

## Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each remediation function
- [ ] E2E test: break tool, run doctor --fix, verify fixed
- [ ] Test --dry-run shows correct actions
- [ ] Test unsafe operations fall back to manual

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (add remediate functions)
- scripts/commands/doctor.sh (add --fix flag)
- New: scripts/lib/remediation.sh (shared remediation helpers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:11.394425192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:50.786428028Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:50.786408852Z","close_reason":"acfs doctor --fix already fully implemented: doctor_fix.sh with 6 fixers (path ordering, config copy, DCG hook, symlink create, plugin clone, ACFS sourcing), dry-run mode, undo support via autofix.sh, 25 passing unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3opa","title":"Deep exploration: Brenner Bot","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of Brenner Bot and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify Brenner installation
[[ -d /dp/brenner_bot ]] && echo "PASS: brenner repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: brenner repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v brenner &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: brenner command available" || { echo "FAIL: brenner not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check corpus location
[[ -d ~/.local/share/brenner/corpus ]] && echo "INFO: Corpus exists" || echo "INFO: No corpus yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/brenner-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/brenner_bot/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for research methodology docs, corpus format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Research session management
  - Hypothesis tracking system
  - Corpus organization and search
  - Multi-agent research swarms
  - Integration with Claude Code

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
brenner --help 2>&1 | head -20
brenner search --help 2>&1 | head -10
brenner hypothesis --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
brenner list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'brenner bot OR research session OR hypothesis'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'brenner research' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/brenner_bot/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/brenner_bot && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Research session structure
- [ ] Hypothesis tracking format
- [ ] Corpus organization
- [ ] Search capabilities
- [ ] Multi-agent coordination

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cass - session mining
- [ ] xf - external research
- [ ] mail - research coordination
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent research

### 2.3 Corpus Verification
```bash
# Check corpus structure
ls -la ~/.local/share/brenner/corpus/ 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "No corpus yet"

# Check hypothesis tracking
brenner hypotheses 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No hypotheses command"
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update brenner entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Research session management
- `description`: Hypothesis-driven research
- `deepDescription`: How research workflow works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test brenner entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const brenner = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'brenner');
console.log('Testing brenner entry...');
console.assert(brenner, 'brenner entry exists');
console.assert(brenner.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(brenner.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Research Workflow
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/brenner-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== Brenner E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG
```

+{"id":"bd-3te83","title":"Add ARM64 Linux binary to meta_skill releases","description":"meta_skill v0.1.0 release is missing aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary. When acfs doctor runs on ARM64 Linux (e.g., AWS Graviton, Raspberry Pi), the install script tries to download ms-0.1.0-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz which 404s.

Current release binaries:
- aarch64-apple-darwin (ARM64 macOS) ✓
- x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu (x86_64 Linux) ✓
- x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (Windows) ✓
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu (ARM64 Linux) ✗ MISSING

Fix:
1. In meta_skill repo: add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to release build matrix (cross-compilation or CI runner)
2. In ACFS: update acfs doctor to inform ARM64 Linux users about workaround until binary is published
3. Cut new meta_skill release with ARM64 Linux binary included

Note: Requires changes in meta_skill repo (upstream), not just ACFS. May need cross-compilation setup.

 {"id":"bd-3ukl","title":"Deep exploration: DCG (Destructive Command Guard)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of DCG (Destructive Command Guard) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify DCG installation
[[ -d /dp/destructive_command_guard ]] && echo "PASS: dcg repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: dcg repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v dcg &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: dcg command available" || { echo "FAIL: dcg not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check if dcg is integrated with shell
grep -q dcg ~/.bashrc ~/.zshrc 2>/dev/null && echo "INFO: dcg in shell config" || echo "INFO: dcg shell integration unclear"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/dcg-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/destructive_command_guard/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for pattern docs, whitelist configuration

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Command interception mechanism (preexec hook? wrapper?)
  - Dangerous command patterns (rm -rf, git reset --hard, etc.)
  - Whitelist/blacklist configuration
  - Integration with Claude Code hooks
  - Escape hatch for legitimate use

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
dcg --help 2>&1 | head -20
dcg status --help 2>&1 | head -10
dcg whitelist --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality (safe test)
dcg status 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 Pattern Verification
```bash
# Check what patterns are blocked
cat /dp/destructive_command_guard/patterns/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Or check code for pattern definitions
grep -r "rm -rf\|git reset\|DROP TABLE" /dp/destructive_command_guard/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

### 1.5 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'dcg OR destructive command OR guard'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'dcg blocked command' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.6 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/destructive_command_guard/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/destructive_command_guard && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Command interception mechanism
- [ ] Complete list of blocked patterns
- [ ] Whitelist configuration format
- [ ] Claude Code hooks integration
- [ ] Override/escape mechanism

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] slb - two-person rule escalation
- [ ] mail - alert messaging
- [ ] ntm - agent coordination

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Count blocked patterns
grep -c "pattern\|regex" /dp/destructive_command_guard/patterns/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{sum+=} END {print "Total patterns: " sum}'

# Verify hook mechanism
grep -l "preexec\|precmd" /dp/destructive_command_guard/**/*.{sh,zsh} 2>/dev/null | head -3
```
